pending.body: Affected: `src/cli/project.rs:1664-1725,1869-1872`. The branch adds alias-aware stable-ID matching (`milestone_bead_refs_match` / `canonicalize_milestone_bead_ref`), but the actual `project create-from-bead` entry path still treats any dotted bead ref as an exact string. `load_bead_detail` only retries `br show --no-db` for refs without dots, and `select_matching_bead_from_show_response` requires `bead.id == bead_id` whenever the query contains a dot. That means a valid short dotted alias like `8.5.3` will still be rejected if `br show` returns `9ni.8.5.3` (or vice versa), so the new alias support is not reachable end-to-end from the CLI. On top of that, `ensure_bead_belongs_to_milestone` still hard-checks `canonical_bead_id.starts_with("<milestone>.")`, which rejects the numeric-milestone alias case that `milestone_bead_refs_match` explicitly accepts. The lookup and membership checks need to use the same milestone-aware alias matcher for dotted refs, not the old exact-string gate.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 3 -> Plan and Implement
